title: Add Dictionary vs Migration View Toggle on Select Page
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to toggle between Dictionary and Migration views on the select page, so that I can use the data dictionary independently without a migration project.
description:
Add a Dictionary vs Migration selector on select.php, similar to the existing toggle on object.php. This enables users to access the dictionary as a standalone feature.

## Dictionary View Columns
- Objects | Metrics | Notes | Alerts
- Object column shows clickable Salesforce object names
- Metrics column placeholder (future feature wiring)
- Notes column reuses existing migration text input
- Alerts column placeholder (future change review counts)

## Additional Changes
- Rename "New SFDC Sink Object" to "Objects" on both Dictionary and Migration views
- Hide inapplicable columns and edit form fields when toggling between views
acceptance_criteria: Dictionary/Migration toggle is available on select.php
Dictionary view shows Objects, Metrics, Notes, and Alerts columns
Objects column contains clickable Salesforce object names
Metrics and Alerts columns are empty placeholders for future functionality
Notes column displays the same text input as migration view
Column header changes from 'New SFDC Sink Object' to 'Objects'
Edit form hides migration-specific fields when in Dictionary view
ux_changes: New Dictionary/Migration toggle on select.php with view-specific column visibility. Dictionary view shows a simplified four-column layout. Edit form fields adjust based on the active view.
implementation_notes: View toggle follows the same pattern as the object.php implementation. Edit form fields are hidden/shown via JavaScript based on active view. Column visibility is toggled client-side.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the toggle and submitted for review. Noted that hiding migration fields from the edit form required additional time.
